name: Shanghai
author: Kronifer
description:
上海 (Shanghai)

SHA · v1.0.0

Detailed map of the prefecture-level city of 上海 (Shanghai) based off data from the 2023 5th National Economic Census.

Coverage

Playable Area: 6,087 km²

Population Summary

Total Population: 24,874,500

Modeled Demand: 13,605,920

Map Statistics

Buildings Indexed: 1,522,626

Demand Points: 23,686

Populations: 150,452

Mean Point Size: 574

Median Point Size: 121

Mean Pop Size: 90

Median Pop Size: 82

Special Demand

Airports

  • Shanghai Hongqiao International Airport: 105,139 daily arrivals/departures modeled

  • Shanghai Pudong International Airport: 147,268 daily arrivals/departures modeled

Universities

  • Tongji University: 37,492 students modeled

  • Fudan University: 31,900 students modeled

  • Jiao-Tong University: 37,989 students modeled

  • Donghua University: 28,497 students modeled

  • SUFE: 18,500 students modeled

  • SISU: 12,395 students modeled

  • ECUST: 25,000 students modeled

  • ECNU: 32,000 students modeled

Additional Features

  • Building Collision — A buildings index is included for all non-filtered buildings, assigning each a flat foundation depth of −10 m in-game. This provides underground collision geometry so that tunnels interact correctly with building footprints.

  • Neighborhood Labels — Three layers of place name labels are embedded in the map:

    • City labels — Prefecture-Level Cities, Districts

    • Municipality labels — Subdistricts, Counties, County-Level Cities

    • Neighborhood labels — Towns, Townships, Neighbourhoods, Villages

Methodology

This map was created using a multi-step methodology, sourcing data from all 16 Districts of Shanghai.

Data Collection

5th Economic Census data was fetched from each individual District of Shanghai. Specifically, Communique/Bulletin no. 1, focusing on employees per subdistrict was obtained.

Spatial Assignment

Population and Employee data for each subdistrict based on the above bulletins were assigned to the geometry of each subdistrict using a Shapefile.

Geographical Processing - Stage 1

Data was disaggregated to the individual building level by assigning a building a usage metric based off of total commercial/residential volume within a Subdistrict.

Geographical Processing - Stage 2

Data was aggregated up to a hexagonal grid with side length n=300m for commute modelling.

Commute Modelling

O/D pairs were created using a Gravity Model, the source for which can be found in the sources linked below.

Map Data

Map Data was fetched from OpenStreetMap, Overture, and 3D-GloBFP and processed by RSlurry's depot package.

Sources

All data used in the creation of this map was obtained from freely available sources.
